docs.title: SpawnerSilk Documentation
docs.text: Mine, manage, and customize mob spawners on Bukkit-based servers. This documentation covers installation, configuration, commands, and developer integration.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text: Get Started Download Configuration

docs.title: Community
docs.text:
• Discord support
  • GitHub repository
  • CurseForge page
  • Bukkit page

docs.title: API
docs.text: SpawnerSilk exposes a small API through the SpawnerAPI class.

docs.title: Create a Spawner Item from an Entity Type
docs.text:
public static ItemStack getSpawner(EntityType entity)

Returns a spawner item for the given entity type.

If the entity type is invalid or unsupported, the method returns a fallback spawner item with a placeholder display name.

Example:

SpawnerAPI.getSpawner(EntityType.SKELETON_HORSE);

docs.title: Read the Entity Type from a Spawner Item
docs.text:
public static EntityType getEntityType(ItemStack is)

Returns the EntityType represented by the given spawner item.

If the item is invalid or unsupported, the method returns EntityType.UNKNOWN.

Example:

SpawnerAPI.getEntityType(myItemStackSpawner);

docs.title: Create a Spawner Item from an Entity Name
docs.text:
public static ItemStack stringToCustomItemStack(String mobName, int amount)

Returns a spawner item for the given mob name and amount.

Example:

SpawnerAPI.stringToCustomItemStack("cat", 1);

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Add the SpawnerSilk jar to your plugin project before calling the API.
  • The exact item output depends on the entity type support available in SpawnerSilk.

docs.title: Commands and Permissions
docs.text: Make sure SpawnerSilk is already installed.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Permission Description /givespawner <player> <type> [amount] spawnersilk.givespawner Gives a spawner to a target player /editspawner <property> [value] spawnersilk.editspawner Edits the spawner block you are looking at /sps reload spawnersilk.reload Reloads the plugin configuration and localization

docs.title: General Permission
docs.text: Permission Description spawnersilk.minespawner Allows players to mine spawners spawnersilk.overlay Allows players to view spawner overlay information

docs.title: /editspawner Properties
docs.text:
To use /editspawner, you must be looking directly at a spawner block.

Supported properties:

  • spawnRange
  • spawnCount
  • maxNearbyEntities
  • requiredPlayerRange
  • delay
  • maxSpawnDelay
  • minSpawnDelay

Example:

/editspawner requiredPlayerRange 5

This makes the spawner active when a player is within five blocks.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Command feedback is localized through the files in localization/.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Make sure SpawnerSilk is already installed before editing the configuration.

SpawnerSilk uses a structured config.yml with sections grouped by feature. After any change, run /sps reload.

docs.title: Main Sections
docs.text: Section Purpose localization Selects the language file loaded from the localization/ folder updates Controls automatic update downloads mining Defines Silk Touch and pickaxe requirements drops Controls drop mode, drop chances, and inventory behavior interaction Controls spawn egg interaction with spawners experience Enables or disables XP drops from mined spawners overlay Configures the spawner overlay display feedback Toggles player-facing messages for break, place, and interact actions restrictions Defines restricted spawner types, such as the blacklist

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
localization:
  language: en_us

mining:
  require-silk-touch-to-break: false
  require-silk-touch-to-drop: true
  required-pickaxe-tier: 5

drops:
  mode: 0
  spawner-chance: 100
  to-inventory: false

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• Missing keys are restored from the default config during updates.
  • The plugin preserves user values while rebuilding the file from the bundled template.

docs.title: Common Settings
docs.text: Key Type Default Description localization.language string en_us Language file to load updates.auto-download boolean true Automatically download supported updates mining.require-silk-touch-to-break boolean false Requires Silk Touch to break a spawner mining.require-silk-touch-to-drop boolean true Requires Silk Touch for a spawner item to drop mining.required-pickaxe-tier integer 5 Minimum pickaxe tier required to collect a spawner drops.mode integer 0 Drop a typed spawner, or a base spawner plus egg drops.spawner-chance integer 100 Chance for a spawner to drop when broken drops.egg-chance integer 100 Chance for a spawn egg to drop in mode 1 drops.explosion-chance integer 10 Chance for a spawner to drop after an explosion drops.to-inventory boolean false Sends drops directly to inventory drops.allow-in-creative boolean false Allows spawner drops in Creative mode interaction.allow-egg-modification boolean true Allows spawn eggs to retune spawners interaction.consume-egg boolean true Consumes the spawn egg after use experience.drop-from-spawners boolean false Drops XP when a spawner is mined overlay.enabled boolean true Enables the spawner overlay overlay.duration-seconds integer 10 Overlay display duration feedback.break-errors boolean true Shows break-related error messages feedback.place-success boolean false Shows a message after placing a spawner feedback.interact-errors boolean true Shows interaction-related error messages feedback.interact-success boolean false Shows a message after changing a spawner restrictions.blacklist list ["BOAT_SPAWNER"] Spawner types that can never be collected

docs.title: Contributing on GitHub
docs.text:
Pull requests are welcome. When submitting changes, explain what you changed and why.

If you plan to work on something substantial, opening an issue or discussion first is usually the best approach.

docs.title: Supporting the Project
docs.text: If you want to support the project financially, you can make a donation on PayPal.

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
You can download SpawnerSilk from the official project pages below:

  • BukkitDev
  • CurseForge
  • GitHub for source code

Warning

If you find SpawnerSilk on another platform, avoid downloading it there unless you trust the source.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
SpawnerSilk supports Minecraft 1.12+ and runs on Java 8+.

Supported server software includes:

  • Bukkit
  • Spigot
  • Paper
  • Purpur
  • Tuinity
  • Most Bukkit/Spigot-compatible forks

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Make sure you have already downloaded the plugin.

docs.title: Install the Plugin
docs.text:
1. Stop your server.
  2. Place the SpawnerSilk-X.X.X.jar file into your plugins/ folder.
  3. Start the server.

If the plugin loads correctly, you should see a message similar to:

[SpawnerSilk] Enabling SpawnerSilk vX.X.X

docs.title: After the First Start
docs.text:
• A default config.yml will be generated in the plugin data folder.
  • The default localization file will be created in plugins/SpawnerSilk/localization/.
  • You can reload configuration changes later with /sps reload.

If the plugin fails to start, check the server version, Java version, and console logs first. If needed, ask for help on Discord.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: This page covers the most common issues you may run into while using SpawnerSilk.

docs.title: The Plugin Does Not Start
docs.text:
Check the following first:

  • Your server version is supported
  • Your Java version matches the plugin requirements
  • The server console does not show dependency or API errors

docs.title: Configuration Changes Are Not Applied
docs.text:
Run:

/sps reload

SpawnerSilk reloads both the main configuration and localization files through this command.

docs.title: Commands Do Not Work
docs.text:
Check:

  • The player has the required permission
  • The command syntax is correct
  • The target spawner or player exists

docs.title: Spawners Do Not Drop
docs.text:
Review these settings in config.yml:

  • mining.require-silk-touch-to-break
  • mining.require-silk-touch-to-drop
  • mining.required-pickaxe-tier
  • drops.spawner-chance
  • drops.allow-in-creative
  • restrictions.blacklist

docs.title: Still Need Help?
docs.text: Ask for help on the project Discord or open an issue on GitHub.
